apex_basket.value_chain_position: Advanced-fab layer — the world's sole source of leading-edge logic (3nm/2nm) every AI accelerator, custom ASIC and smartphone AP must be built on; ~60% global foundry share and ~100% of sub-5nm capacity.
apex_basket.vector_evidence: The AI-compute secular force flows THROUGH TSM as the irreplaceable chokepoint: every accelerator (NVDA GPU, hyperscaler ASIC, AMD MI-series) is fabbed here, so more AI compute = more leading-edge wafers + richer CoWoS/SoIC mix — gross margin rose 54.4%->66.2% (FY23->Q1'26) INTO the ramp, the toll-rate widening not eroding.
apex_basket.thesis: The supply-chain chokepoint of the entire AI build-out and the book anchor with INTU vetoed — N2 sold out plus rising scarcity rent give the cleanest live earnings-momentum path among the AI-capex legs; combined-capped with NVDA.
apex_basket.theme_durability: ai_infrastructure — revenue +31% YoY (+36% USD on the prior window), N2 sold out, demand visibility two tiers deep; Intel 18A years behind and Samsung trails on yield — the most structural moat in the book (load_bearing 5, interrogator 5).
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: gross margin 54.4%->56.1%->59.9%->66.2% (FY23->Q1'26) on +30%+ revenue, beating its own 63-65% guide by 120bps — rising scarcity rent / pricing power; only deliberate overseas-fab (2-4%) + N2-ramp (2-3% in 2H'26) dilution as offsets.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: 30%+ ROIC-class returns, NT$1.1T TTM FCF growing through a CapEx super-cycle, net cash (net-funded-debt/EBITDA -0.69); rule-of-40 = 57.8.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: Base SoP ~$1,180/ADR sits ABOVE the screen-quoted ~$424 ADR (the SoP capitalizes local EPS x ADR ratio); guard passes — size is held by the AI-capex combined cap, not the guard.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — load_bearing 5, interrogator 5, conviction 4, STRENGTHENING; customer concentration (Apple/NVDA at the leading node) + the Taiwan geopolitical single-point-of-failure are the structural tails behind the combined cap.
apex_basket.goal_note: 12-mo driver = the live earnings inflection — gross margin running 59.9%->66.2% on +30%+ revenue with N2 fully booked; the ADR compounds with EPS and +25-35% inside a year is consistent with the GM/volume ramp — the highest-confidence near-term mover of the AI-capex pair.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: AI-accelerator silicon + the CUDA software platform — the dominant compute layer every LLM training/inference workload must run on; ~80-90% of AI-training revenue.
apex_basket.vector_evidence: NVDA IS the secular force: the AI-compute paradigm is being built ON CUDA/Blackwell, so every incremental token/model/sovereign cluster expands its TAM (DC revenue +142% then +65-71%) — gross margin EXPANDED to ~75% into the largest ramp in tech history, the opposite of a disrupted incumbent.
apex_basket.thesis: The most load-bearing chokepoint in the universe and the compute leg of the AI-capex axis — the Blackwell/Rubin ramp gives a live +38% bull path; combined-capped with TSM because they share the hyperscaler-capex driver and measured 0.63 correlated.
apex_basket.theme_durability: ai_infrastructure — DC revenue +68%, backlog frame widened (Blackwell+Rubin), demand broadening from CSP to sovereign/enterprise/networking; secular but the demand is one capex line that can digest 1-2 quarters.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: non-GAAP gross margin rose 71%->~75% WHILE revenue grew +114% then +65% — durable pricing power; management called a GM trough and recovery two quarters out and hit it.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: Elite ~45% ROIC on incremental capital, 47% FCF margin, $119B TTM FCF, net cash (net-funded-debt/EBITDA ~0.0); capex-light fabless model redeploys at extreme returns; rule-of-40 = 117.6.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos -0.3% — base SoP ~$210 sits on top of the ~$211 price (no discount); bull SoP ~$283 = +34%; peak_flag true (the air-pocket case) — guard does not veto but argues for the combined-capped size.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high but cycle-sensitive — load_bearing 5, interrogator 5, STRENGTHENING; the top-5 hyperscalers are ~50% of DC revenue and also build competing ASICs — a 2-quarter digestion pause is the central risk and the reason for the combined cap.
apex_basket.goal_note: 12-mo driver = the Blackwell/GB-series ramp carrying DC revenue and a rising ~75% GM; bull SoP +34% is a live-momentum path within the year. Held at the AI-capex combined cap (not above it) because the same hyperscaler-capex digestion that powers the +34% is also the -34% air-pocket risk (peak_flag true).

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Vertically integrated uranium miner/processor — Tier-1 assets (Cigar Lake, McArthur River) plus conversion and the Westinghouse stake; the nuclear-fuel chokepoint for the existing fleet and the SMR build-out.
apex_basket.vector_evidence: The energy-transition + AI-baseload force GROWS uranium demand against a structural supply deficit ('the risk to supply is far greater than the risk to demand'); CCJ is the highest-quality toll-taker whose long-term contract book re-prices UP into the deficit — the disruption flows toward it, not against it.
apex_basket.thesis: The highest-quality public uranium toll-taker and the book's cleanest orthogonal reach — a live contract-re-pricing + AI-baseload-demand re-rate that does NOT ride the hyperscaler capex line, with bull SoP ~$133-143 = +32-42% inside the horizon.
apex_basket.theme_durability: energy_transition / nuclear-fuel supply — long-term contract prices re-pricing to the highest since 2008, AI-baseload demand plus the Westinghouse program; load_bearing 5, interrogator 5, conviction 3.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector AMBER: consolidated gross margin COMPRESSED 33.9%->26.7% (FY24->FY25) on cost inflation / Inkai sourcing even as realized contract prices rose — pricing power is in contract prices, not yet in consolidated GM, which must re-expand as higher-priced contracts roll on.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: 25% FCF margin, ~C$1.9B FY25 adj-EBITDA, net cash (net-funded-debt/EBITDA -0.1); incremental returns ride re-pricing of the long-term book plus Westinghouse distributions; rule-of-40 = 32.9.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: Base SoP ~$95-100 brackets the ~$101 quote (~flat); guard passes but the amber GM keeps size disciplined at 1.0.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — STRENGTHENING, latest-Q EPS YoY +94%, contract-backlog re-pricing delivered verbatim across quarters; GM-compression + an undatable utility-driven re-rate clock are the watch-items.
apex_basket.goal_note: 12-mo driver = higher-priced long-term contracts rolling onto the book re-expanding consolidated GM, plus uranium-spot momentum on AI-baseload demand; bull SoP +32-42% is a live trend, not 'eventually' — the orthogonal reach that lets the book chase the goal without adding AI-capex beta.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Spine-surgery implants + the ExcelsiusGPS robotic-navigation platform — capital equipment drives per-procedure implant consumable pull-through; the largest pure-play spine platform post-NuVasive.
apex_basket.vector_evidence: Surgical-robotics adoption is the secular force and GMED is the share-GAINING attacker, not the disrupted incumbent: ExcelsiusGPS takes share from Medtronic Mazor / Stryker, the robot is the razor pulling through high-margin implants — six straight quarters of GM expansion confirm the toll widening as share is gained in a low-single-digit market.
apex_basket.thesis: A guard-clean robotics leg with an expanding-margin implant annuity and a live placement-driven pull-through inflection — bull SoP $132 = +39% — diversifying the book into surgical robotics orthogonal to the AI-capex line.
apex_basket.theme_durability: robotics_automation / surgical robotics — ExcelsiusGPS taking share via competitive-rep recruiting + robot->implant pull-through; load_bearing 4, revenue +23.5% YoY (42% 3yr CAGR incl. NuVasive).
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: gross margin ~67.4% rising for six straight quarters, base-business adj-EBITDA back to mid-30s (35.7% Q4'25) on +16.7% revenue — pricing power, not commoditization.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: Reported ROIC ~6.2% (depressed by ~$3.6B NuVasive goodwill, normalizing as fixed-cost infrastructure leverages — judge on INCREMENTAL capital), net cash (net-funded-debt/EBITDA -0.5), $605M TTM FCF; rule-of-40 = 43.0.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos +13.6% / ev_gp 5.11x vs +23% rev — guard passes clean (one of the cheapest gross-profit multiples in the book; the +134% scan FV is a Graham-multiplier artifact, discounted).
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — STRENGTHENING, interrogator 4; watch the 6.2% reported-ROIC normalization, the Enabling-Tech shift to operating leases, and Medtronic / Stryker next-gen robotics competition.

wheel.rationale: Own it lower at the thesis break, cap the re-rate at base fair value while the integration-suppressed ROIC normalizes.

apex_basket.goal_note: 12-mo driver = NuVasive-integration margin normalization (six straight quarters of GM expansion) plus ExcelsiusGPS placements pulling through high-margin implants; bull SoP +39% off a still-positive +13.6% base MoS is a credible within-year earnings-inflection path.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Diversified medtech — cardiovascular, neuromodulation, urology and endoscopy devices including pulsed-field-ablation (Farawave) and the Ion robotic-bronchoscopy platform; per-procedure consumable pull-through on a robotics_automation razor/blade.
apex_basket.vector_evidence: BSX is the PFA share leader riding a fast-growing secular shift in cardiac ablation — Farawave gains share from Medtronic/J&J, the procedure-volume force grows its consumable annuity; this is a share-gaining attacker, not a disrupted incumbent (the watch-item is competitive-entry margin pressure, not displacement of the franchise).
apex_basket.thesis: The deepest guard pass in the eligible set, seated as a downside-PROTECTED reach — bear SoP ~$62 sits ABOVE the ~$45 price with sop_mos +72% — a PFA/EP + robotic-bronchoscopy grower whose only blemish is a first-guided GM step-down (amber, not red), sized 0.9 for that amber.
apex_basket.theme_durability: robotics_automation / structural-heart + EP — Farawave PFA is the fastest-growing segment in cardiac ablation and a share-gainer; Ion robotic bronchoscopy adds a second robotics leg; load_bearing 4, revenue +17.4% YoY.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector AMBER (the only blemish): gross margin ~69% FY25 with adj op-margin walked to 28%, BUT management GUIDED FY26 gross margin 'slightly lower' on adverse high-margin EP/WATCHMAN mix — first guided compression after years of expansion as PFA competition (J&J Varipulse, Abbott Volt) enters; net margin still rising and FCF strong.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: $3.44B TTM FCF (16.7% FCF margin), net-funded leverage 2.0x with 11.6x coverage; incremental returns ride PFA capital placements -> consumable pull-through; rule-of-40 = 34.1.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos +72.2% / ev_gp 5.84x — guard passes DEEP; the adverse-case SoP ~$62 is +32% ABOVE the ~$45 price, so the name is structurally downside-cushioned even in a 12-18mo theme pause.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — interrogator 4, but trajectory PIVOTING and the FY26 GM guide-down is the watch-item (transient mix air-pocket vs structural PFA competition?); the deep guard cushion is what earns the seat despite the amber.

wheel.rationale: Deep guard + bear-FV-above-price makes this an ideal wheel: get paid below the thesis break, cap at the +72% base fair value while the amber FY26 GM guide plays out.

apex_basket.goal_note: 12-mo driver = Farawave PFA share gains + the CHAMPION-AF readout potentially re-accelerating WATCHMAN, against a base SoP $78 that is +72% above spot; the rare goal-fit name where upside (+40-72%) and downside protection (bear FV above price) BOTH hold — sized 0.9 only for the amber FY26 GM guide.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Restaurant-vertical payment OS — integrated POS hardware + payment processing + SaaS for restaurants; takes a toll on every card swipe and subscription seat in the food-service stack.
apex_basket.vector_evidence: TOST is the share-GAINING vertical disruptor, not a disrupted incumbent: it is digitizing an under-penetrated restaurant market (~20% of US SMB restaurants, doubled in 3 years), take rate RISING +2bps to 51bps and SaaS GM crossed 81% — the fintech-rails/vertical-software force flows through it (the DoorDash-POS entry is a forward competitive risk, not a secular force eroding its still-expanding pricing power).
apex_basket.thesis: Guard-clean, goal-fit restaurant-vertical payment OS on the apex via the INTU veto + the robotics-theme diversify-swap — GM expanding, net income inflected positive, net-cash share-gainer with bull SoP $42 = +73%.
apex_basket.theme_durability: fintech_rails / vertical-payments — restaurant-specific workflow depth (KDS, online ordering, payroll) horizontal POS can't replicate; 31% 3-yr CAGR, ToastIQ/agentic-platform + Toast Local extending the moat; load_bearing 4.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: SaaS gross margin crossed 81% (+~300bps) and blended GM 25.8% rising on +22-24% revenue, take rate +2bps to 51bps (Q1'26) — expanding GM on growing revenue = genuine pricing power; net income inflected -246M -> +19M -> +342M.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: 10% FCF margin, $654M TTM FCF (+98.7% YoY), net cash (net-funded-debt/EBITDA -2.46, Altman-Z 10.5) to out-invest subscale rivals; incremental returns ride location adds + platform attach; rule-of-40 = 33.6.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos +25.7% / ev_gp 7.56x vs +23% rev — guard passes clean (bear SoP $22 is net-cash-floored, ~-10%).
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — STRENGTHENING, interrogator 4; the load-bearing watch-item is DoorDash bundling a subsidized POS into the largest US restaurant demand channel + GPV/location softness (macro-levered SMB restaurant economy).
apex_basket.goal_note: 12-mo driver = continued location adds + take-rate/attach expansion on a profit-inflected base; bull SoP $42 = +73%, base SoP $32 = +31% off a net-cash-floored bear at ~-10% — a goal-fit reach whose downside is balance-sheet-protected; the DoorDash-POS overhang holds the size to 0.9.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Global payment-network operator — VisaNet connects ~15,000 issuers to ~100M merchant locations, taking a per-transaction toll on every card-based payment globally; the canonical fintech-rails chokepoint.
apex_basket.vector_evidence: Net beneficiary on the evidence: the cash->digital secular force GROWS Visa's volume, net revenue ACCELERATED +9%->+17% (the rare accelerating mega-cap) and the ~30% VAS software mix deepens the moat; Visa is co-opting the disintermediation threat by OWNING the stablecoin/agentic on-ramp (stablecoin programs, Intelligent Commerce live) — the tailwind mechanism is affirmable, so it clears the gate, but the contested overhang argues for ballast sizing.
apex_basket.thesis: A load-bearing duopoly rail with retained pricing power and a VAS software re-rate, sized DOWN to ballast under the goal because its 12-mo upside (+20% bull / at-spot base) is the smallest of the slate — the orthogonal rate-duration anchor, not a reach.
apex_basket.theme_durability: fintech_rails — duopoly network effect no entrant has broken at scale; net revenue +9%->+17%, ~30% VAS mix compounding mid-20s.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: ~65-67% GAAP operating margin retained while net revenue accelerated and EPS +35% latest-Q — pricing power retained even as lower-margin VAS/Visa-Direct mixed in.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: 49% FCF margin, $21.2B TTM FCF; VAS (~30% of revenue, software-grade margins) is the incremental-return engine; rule-of-40 = 63.6; net-funded-debt/EBITDA 0.41 funds the buyback (a small liability, not a kicker).
apex_basket.valuation_guard: Base SoP ~$300-305 sits just below the ~$320 quote (~-5%); guard passes with no size penalty — the small size reflects the goal (modest 12-mo upside), not a guard deduction.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — load_bearing 5, interrogator 5, STRENGTHENING; the structural watch-item is the disintermediation overhang (stablecoin/agentic fee-avoidance + a floated US rate-cap) — an overhang Visa is racing to own rather than a realized erosion.
apex_basket.goal_note: Does NOT clear the +30-50% bar in 12 months (bull SoP ~$385 = +20%, base at-spot) — kept as the lowest-volatility rate-duration ballast that anchors the book's downside in a NEUTRAL/late-cycle regime; a quality compounder earning a smaller seat, not a reach.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Veterinary-diagnostics toll-taker — in-clinic analyzers + recurring consumable test kits + a direct reference-lab network; a razor/blade installed base deeply sticky once placed.
apex_basket.vector_evidence: The diagnostics-intensification secular force flows through IDXX: it GROWS the category (frequency + tests-per-visit) and out-executes a declining-visit end-market, widening its premium-to-visits with new instruments (inVue Dx, Cancer Dx) — pricing power + utilization, the textbook beneficiary signature, not a disrupted incumbent.
apex_basket.thesis: An orthogonal, guard-clean compounder with 36% ROIC and an expanding-margin razor/blade moat — sized DOWN to ballast because its 12-mo upside (+18% bull / +9.4% base) is modest, but it remains the book's purest diversifier away from every shared axis.
apex_basket.theme_durability: genomics_bio_tools / companion-animal diagnostics — secular pet-health spend + green shoots from aging pandemic-adopted pets, no scaled public competitor, durable consumable annuity per placed instrument with stacking switching-cost layers (Velo software).
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: comparable gross margin 61.8%->63.4% (FY25->Q1'26, +90bps) on +10-12% organic revenue, premium-to-visits widening over a declining end-market — pricing power, not commoditization.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: Elite 36% ROIC (return on incremental capital), +10-12% organic with double-digit international runway, rule-of-40 = 37.4 and 24% FCF margin.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos +9.4% / ev_gp 16.9x vs +13% rev — guard passes (price ~$562 sits below base SoP ~$615); no deduction, though the ~43x P/FCF leaves no cushion if the visit premium compresses.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — load_bearing 5, interrogator 5, STRENGTHENING; no AI-capex, China, or single-customer exposure — the most orthogonal name in the book; the only risk is quality-vs-price (43x FCF into a still-declining visit base).

wheel.rationale: A slow at-fair-value compounder you would happily own lower: sell the put at the thesis break, cover-call the modest re-rate at base fair value.

apex_basket.goal_note: Does NOT clear +30-50% in 12 months (bull SoP $685 = +18%, base +9.4%) — earns a smaller seat as the single fully-orthogonal (zero shared-axis) 36%-ROIC compounder, the diversification ballast the high-beta AI-capex reaches require; a longer-horizon hold, not a 12-mo mover.

runner_ups.vector_evidence: AI deepens the moat: PTC embeds agents on proprietary product-lifecycle data 'no general-purpose AI model can reach', turning systems-of-record into systems-of-action — the AI force is monetization upside, not displacement; 83.8% expanding GM + competitive-displacement wins confirm the beneficiary sign.
runner_ups.thesis: Cheap (ev_gp 6.8), guard-clean (sop_mos +14.6), expanding ~84%-GM industrial-software/PLM toll with a goal-fit bull path (+45-58%); the highest-scoring guard-clean Tech beneficiary, held off the apex ONLY because the robotics_automation theme + 3-per-Technology GICS caps bind (it was the diversify-swap loser to TOST, which sits in a different theme). First in if a robotics or Tech slot frees.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos +14.6% / ev_gp 6.77x vs +28% rev — guard passes clean, but headline rev (+28%) overstates the ~8.5% ARR truth; the re-acceleration rests partly on unproven deferred-ARR.

runner_ups.vector_evidence: Beneficiary on the bookings evidence: NOW sells the AI-agent orchestration layer / system-of-record AI agents run THROUGH (Now Assist ACV >$600M, 30%+ AI price uplift, RPO +23.5%) — the 'AI eats SaaS' force is the bear narrative its own data refutes, not a realized headwind; but flat 3-yr GAAP EPS + sub-7% ROIC keep it off the apex.
runner_ups.thesis: Guard-clean enterprise-workflow + AI-agent orchestration toll (GM flat-high 77.5%, op-margin expanding, sop_mos +24.2); the de-rate is a multiple-detachment, not fundamentals — but the contested AI-displacement overhang (NRR undisclosed, organic decel to ~19%) + the Technology GICS cap hold it on the bench.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos +24.2% / ev_gp 9.9x vs +22% rev — guard passes clean.

runner_ups.vector_evidence: Beneficiary: 91% workflow-monopoly GM held flat on +17.5% revenue (no compression = no commoditization), AI framed 'for the physical world' on decades of proprietary CAD data as the moat against generic-AI entrants — the AI force is monetization (neural foundation models, MCP/API) not displacement; the opposite of INTU's horizontal-tooling victim layer.
runner_ups.thesis: 91%-GM AEC/manufacturing CAD workflow monopoly, deepest margin-of-safety in Tech (sop_mos +35.4, bear-SoP ~$212 ABOVE the ~$194 price = downside-protected, bull +36%), activist-validated FY29 margin target; benched behind the robotics_automation theme + Technology GICS caps.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos +35.4% / ev_gp 6.95x vs +18% rev — guard passes clean (bear-SoP sits above the price); 17.3x FCF is a valuation dislocation, not a fundamental one.

runner_ups.vector_evidence: Beneficiary: the AI build-out grows VRT's power/cooling content per MW (backlog doubled, GM expanding on +27.7% rev) — the chokepoint widens with the force; the recurring 'can hyperscalers route around you' question is answered content-additively, not by erosion.
runner_ups.thesis: The power-and-cooling leg of the AI-capex axis and the most guard-borderline of the three AI-infra chokepoints — kept on the bench to honor the AI-capex 30% ceiling (NVDA+TSM already carry it); first back in if an AI-capex slot frees and the cap allows.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos -15.9% — price ~$333 sits above base SoP ~$300; guard DEDUCTS (price above base SoP) and its AI-capex axis membership keeps it benched; P/FCF ~69x is a multiple case, not a value case.

runner_ups.vector_evidence: Beneficiary: the digitization secular force flows through Kaspi's super-app monopoly (~80% of Kazakhstan digital payments, GM 62.3%->73.5% on +56% revenue) — it is the disruptor of cash, not a disrupted incumbent; the cap is macro (18% sovereign rate flat-lined reported NI) + a sanctions tail, not a wrong-sign.
runner_ups.thesis: A load-bearing EM fintech monopoly with expanding GM, high ROE and net cash, trading at a low earnings multiple — but it is a rate-cycle + sovereign-discount trade with a fat sanctions/FX left tail, so it benches behind the cleaner fintech_rails beneficiaries; the rate turn is the live catalyst.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: Base SoP ~$95 vs ~$80 quote (~+18%); guard passes, but the abnormal cheapness is macro-gated (rate cycle) + carries a sovereign/sanctions tail, not a clean disruptor discount.

disruptor_memo.rubric_and_weighting: Four pillars ~25 pts each applied ONLY to names clearing ALL gates INCLUDING the disruption-vector sign: (1) theme durability & load-bearing position, (2) moat & pricing power with the gross-margin trajectory as the lie-detector, (3) reinvestment runway & incremental ROIC, (4) growth-adjusted valuation as a GUARD ONLY. Pillars 1-3 set the ranking; valuation (sop_mos_pct, ev_gp, rule_of_40, peak_flag) acted purely as a guard that could deduct, cap, or veto and was NEVER the reason one name outranked another that passed the guard clean. The signed disruption-vector gate is applied to EVERY name BEFORE the rubric — a name whose theme's secular force is a HEADWIND (a disruptee / wrong-sign) is INELIGIBLE no matter how wide its moat, how clean its trailing GM, or how cheap its multiple. Abnormal cheapness was treated as a WARNING that triggers the vector gate, never as margin of safety.
disruptor_memo.gate_results: All 40 debated names cleared the hard gates as pre-stamped and re-verified: every name is ttm_fcf_positive=true, rev_growth_gate=true, funded_solvency != weak (all strong / moderate / exempt_financial — BE, BWXT, FICO, HII are 'moderate' but not weak), and forensic_gate is empty (no EXCLUDE, no CAP). Differentiation came from the disruption-vector sign gate, the rubric, the valuation guard, and the 12-mo goal overlay.
disruptor_memo.excluded_by_forensic_gate: None — no name carried forensic_gate=EXCLUDE or CAP.
disruptor_memo.excluded_by_hard_gates: None on FCF/growth/solvency — all 40 passed (every name ttm_fcf_positive=true on a trailing basis; even BE, IOT and BLLN print positive TTM FCF). No fcf_inflecting-only name had to be carried, so the <=0.5 inflecting size rule did not bind.

excluded_by_disruption_vector_gate.INTU: DISRUPTEE | loser_theme=ai-displacement. THE LIVE TEST CASE — and it FAILS. Dossier-confirmed: management openly conceded 'lost on price' in DIY TurboTax (FY26 Q3) to free/AI substitutes; revenue decelerated +17-18%->+15% (gm_trajectory note explicitly flags the DIY price war masked only by TurboTax-Live mix). INTU sits in TWO ai-displacement victim_layers ('legal/tax/DIY tooling' + 'horizontal SaaS with thin switching costs'). Its tells are exactly the disruptee signature: a wide moat (85% QuickBooks share), a still-expanding aggregate GM (a LAGGING read masking the DIY price war), and the CHEAPEST multiple in the book (sop_mos +70.4, ev_gp 4.88). The aggregate GM cannot be affirmed as a tailwind mechanism while the DIY engine re-rates to a defended-but-shrinking cash cow. INELIGIBLE — belongs in the VALUE book under its secular_theme cap, not the disruptor sleeve. NOTE: INTU was the PRIOR basket's #1 anchor (1.15 units); the signed gate is exactly what removes it.
excluded_by_disruption_vector_gate.FICO: DISRUPTEE | loser_theme=payments-disintermediation. VantageScore (~$0.99 vs FICO's >$10) winning GSE dual-adoption into FICO's crown-jewel conforming-mortgage channel (mortgage is a large share of Scores revenue, and the growth is PRICE not volume) plus a regulatory pricing probe; verdict C, conviction 2, trajectory PIVOTING — the tell of an incumbent under attack on a forward basis. Two-sided-to-negative sign => disruptee for eligibility; also independently guard-failed (sop_mos -15.2). Belongs in the value book, not here.

disruptor_memo.contested_signs_resolved_beneficiary: Names whose disruption sign is contested but where an affirmative TAILWIND mechanism is stated in vector_evidence, so they clear the gate: V (loser_theme payments-disintermediation tagged — stablecoin/agentic/rate-cap overhang is real, BUT cash->digital still GROWS volume +9->+17%, VAS deepens the moat, and Visa OWNS the stablecoin on-ramp; net beneficiary, sized to ballast for the overhang); NOW (loser_theme ai-displacement tagged — the 'AI eats SaaS' bear is the narrative its own bookings refute; beneficiary on evidence but benched on GICS cap + the unresolved NRR/organic-decel warts); KSPI (payments-disintermediation tagged — it IS the disruptor of cash in its geography; benched on the macro/sovereign tail, not the sign). The INTU contrast is decisive and validates the gate: ADSK/PTC/NOW/TOST are vertical or proprietary-data software where AI is a TAILWIND (they own the data moat AI needs and price the upside), while INTU's horizontal 'good-enough' DIY-tax layer is exactly the ai-displacement victim layer where AI already drew blood — same broad software universe, opposite sign.
disruptor_memo.deducted_or_vetoed_by_valuation_guard: Guard VETO / size-0.5 territory (sop_mos_pct <= -40%, the CRO cannot get near the price): ASML (-99.9), BE (-85.6), HWM (-73.5), ARM (-60.2), WWD (-58.1), PLTR (-79.4). Guard DEDUCTED (price above base SoP, ~-15% to -33%) and kept off the apex: ANET (-29.3, ALSO the clearest moat-amber in the universe — GM compressing as the whales negotiate the toll rate down, the early commoditization signature, peak_flag true), CRDO (-28.3, peak_flag), ISRG (-28.7, verdict C), GE (-32.9, C), HEI (-21.4), MEDP (-19.1, C + rising customer concentration into cancelling metabolic names), WST (-22.3, C), APP (-17.0, peak_flag), MA (quality-at-full-price, no asymmetry), NVT (price above ~$122 SoP, AMBER GM, stale -60% latest-Q EPS), HII (slim +7% to fair, FLAT/cost-plus GM, maturing). Under the GOAL these guard-failures hurt twice: the names with the most aggressive embedded S-curves (ARM, PLTR, CRDO, ASML) are exactly the ones where a +30-50% 12-mo move would require multiple EXPANSION from an already-stretched base — the goal does not rescue a guard veto, and abnormal cheapness (where present) triggered the vector gate rather than counting as comfort.
disruptor_memo.other_misses: BLLN (genomics — guard borderline at sop_mos -3.0 and price already at base SoP; behind IDXX on quality/scale, no goal-fit asymmetry); DLO and BWXT and CCJ-tier energy names that did seat or bench on guard. POWL/FSLR/BWXT/BE in energy_transition all sit behind CCJ on either guard (BE -85.6, BWXT -25.0+C+FLAT cost-plus GM, FSLR -4.9 but theme-loser tariff-dependence) or quality; the single energy seat went to the highest-quality toll-taker (CCJ). GE/HII/HWM/HEI/WWD/BWXT in defense_tech all guard-deducted or vetoed (none seated). PEN (robotics, +15.1 guard-clean but deal-gated, stale -18% latest-Q, behind GMED/BSX). IOT (robotics, guard-deducts on price above SoP, -44% to bear).
disruptor_memo.fcf_inflecting_keeps: None. No apex pick is fcf_inflecting; every one is ttm_fcf_positive=true on a trailing basis, so no name required guidance/backlog FCF-turn evidence or the <=0.5 size cap on that basis.
disruptor_memo.rise_fall_vs_prior_apex: Prior disruptor apex (read from the existing apex_basket_disruptor.json before overwriting) = [TSM(1.05), NVDA(1.00), CCJ(1.00), GMED(1.00), BSX(0.90), TOST(0.90), V(0.85), IDXX(0.75)]. New apex = [TSM(1.05), NVDA(1.00), CCJ(1.00), GMED(1.00), BSX(0.90), TOST(0.90), V(0.85), IDXX(0.75)] — UNCHANGED membership and sizing. The input prices and debate fields are materially identical to the prior run, so the eight names that cleared the signed vector gate + guard + goal overlay last run clear them again; no name's sign, gate, or guard status flipped. RISE/FALL by name: TSM HELD 1.05 (anchor, live GM/earnings momentum intact); NVDA HELD 1.00 (expected_return trimmed 38->34 to the current sop_mos -0.3 base, still combined-capped); CCJ HELD 1.00; GMED HELD 1.00; BSX HELD 0.90 (amber FY26 GM guide); TOST HELD 0.90; V HELD 0.85 (rate-duration ballast, meets_goal=false); IDXX HELD 0.75 (orthogonal ballast, meets_goal=false). INTU and FICO remain VETOED as disruptees (INTU the prior-prior #1 anchor, already removed). Net unit total = 7.45 (unchanged), deliberately slightly smaller/cleaner with the two disruptees out rather than force-filling into a lower-conviction reach (PTC/NOW/ADSK/VRT/KSPI on the bench).

theme_concentration_stress.ai-capex (MANDATORY check): CARRIES 2 names — NVDA (compute) and TSM (foundry/supply-chain) — the two legs of the SAME hyperscaler-capex line; a 2-quarter digestion pause hits both at once and they measured 0.63 correlated (2y weekly, the binding max pair, the ONLY pair >=0.6 in the book). RESOLVED by keep-with-cap: combined_caps {names:[NVDA,TSM], max_units:2.05, axis:'ai-capex'} = 27.5% of the 7.45-unit book, under the 30% ceiling. Argued against the real numbers: at avg pairwise 0.24 and a single 0.63 NVDA-TSM pair, capping the pair at 27.5% means one hyperscaler-capex cut impairs <28% of the book; VRT (the third AI-capex leg, also ~0.6 to the pair) was DIVERSIFIED to the bench rather than seated, so the systematic shock is NOT spread across three positions. The ai_infrastructure THEME id independently caps at 2 names / 27.5% — both constraints satisfied.
theme_concentration_stress.china-export-controls: CARRIES 2 names — NVDA and TSM (both with China revenue + license/geopolitical exposure). RESOLVED = covered by the same ai-capex combined cap (identical membership), so a single export-control ruling is bounded by the 2.05-unit / 27.5% ceiling; no separate swap required.
theme_concentration_stress.supply-chain-tsmc: CARRIES 2 names — TSM (the foundry) and NVDA (fabbed at TSMC); a Taiwan/advanced-node disruption hits both. RESOLVED = bounded by the same ai-capex combined cap (both members), within the 27.5% ceiling.
theme_concentration_stress.single-customer-hyperscaler: CARRIES 2 names — NVDA and TSM ultimately depend on the same handful of hyperscaler buyers (VRT, the third dependent, was diversified out to the bench). RESOLVED = identical membership to the ai-capex cap, so the 2.05-unit / 27.5% ceiling already constrains it.
theme_concentration_stress.rate-duration: CARRIES 2 apex names — V and IDXX (long-duration quality multiples that compress together when real rates rise). RESOLVED = kept WITHOUT a numeric combined cap: they sit across DIFFERENT GICS sectors (Financial Services / Healthcare) and orthogonal sub-drivers (payment network / vet-diagnostics annuity), are both guard-clean, and are deliberately ballast-sized (0.85 / 0.75 = 21.5% combined). A rate shock is a multiple event, not a thesis break, for both; in a NEUTRAL/neutral-rates regime this is the intended defensive ballast against the AI-capex beta.
theme_concentration_stress.medtech-capital-cycle: CARRIES 2 names — GMED and BSX (both ride hospital/surgical capital-equipment placement -> consumable pull-through). RESOLVED = kept WITHOUT a numeric cap: different sub-verticals (spine robotics vs cardiac PFA/EP), sum to 25.5% under the 30% robotics_automation theme cap which binds first, and BSX is the deepest-guarded name in the book (bear FV above price) so the pair's downside is structurally cushioned rather than correlated to a thesis break.
theme_concentration_stress.robotics_automation (theme cap stress): Eligible robotics_automation beneficiaries were GMED, BSX, PTC, NOW, ADSK, IOT, PEN — more than the 3-name cap allows. RESOLVED by DIVERSIFY-SWAP: seated GMED+BSX (2 names / 25.5%, under the 30% theme cap) and pushed the cheaper/cleaner software legs (PTC, ADSK, NOW) to the bench, filling the freed Tech/fintech slot with TOST (fintech_rails) so the book is not robotics-clustered. PTC is the first name back in if a robotics or Tech slot frees.
theme_concentration_stress.vertical-payments / fintech-rails: fintech_rails CARRIES 2 apex names — V (network rail) and TOST (restaurant vertical OS) at 23.5% combined, under the 30% theme cap. Orthogonal sub-drivers (global card duopoly vs US-restaurant SMB digitization) and different GICS sectors (Financial Services vs Technology); no numeric cap required.
theme_concentration_stress.energy_transition / nuclear-fuel: CARRIES 1 apex name — CCJ only (VRT, which carried the energy_transition 2nd theme, is benched). No >=2-name energy axis remains; CCJ's nuclear-fuel-cycle + commodity-spot exposure is idiosyncratic and orthogonal to every other axis in the book.
theme_concentration_stress.gics_sector_check: Technology = TSM, NVDA, TOST = 3 names (at the 3-per-GICS cap, satisfied — this is why PTC/NOW/ADSK could not also seat). Healthcare = GMED, BSX, IDXX = 3 names (at cap). Financial Services = V (1). Energy = CCJ (1). No GICS sector exceeds 3.

disruptor_memo.construction_note: GOAL-DRIVEN RE-PICK UNDER THE SIGNED VECTOR GATE: the decisive filter is the disruption-vector sign gate vetoing INTU and FICO as ai-displacement / payments-disintermediation DISRUPTEES — removed not for valuation but because the secular force is a HEADWIND (INTU 'lost on price' to AI tax tooling; FICO losing the GSE mortgage channel to VantageScore). Under a balanced-leaning-REACH stance (NEUTRAL regime, accelerating growth), sizing tilts toward names with a LIVE 12-month re-rate driver — TSM/NVDA at goal size on earnings momentum (combined-capped at 27.5%), CCJ on uranium contract re-pricing, GMED on placement->implant inflection, BSX as a downside-protected reach (bear FV above price), TOST on take-rate/location compounding. The two lowest-12-mo-upside compounders (V, IDXX, both meets_goal=false) stay ballast. Six of eight apex names clear the +30-50%/12-mo goal (TSM, NVDA, CCJ, GMED, BSX, TOST); V and IDXX are the deliberate orthogonal/rate-duration ballast. Valuation acted only as a guard throughout — the richest hypergrowth disruptors (ARM, ASML, PLTR, APP, CRDO, HWM, WWD) were vetoed/deducted by the guard, ANET was deducted AND flagged moat-amber (whale-driven GM compression), and abnormal cheapness was read as a vector-gate trigger (INTU/FICO) rather than as safety. Net unit total 7.45; the book is deliberately slightly smaller/cleaner with the two disruptees out.

stress_test.note: Market-based stress: weighted basket return to the 52-week lows, and to 52w-lows -15% (recession). cro_bear is the agents' own adverse SoP (bear_fv_px, theme-pause case); when missing or implying upside it is flagged invalid and the published downside is the market-based recession stress.

correlation.fx_note: betas vs SMH (semis) + QQQ (long-duration tech) — the AI-capex/rate-duration systematic axes.

pool_stats.banner: Highest-volatility sleeve: 8 profitable secular-theme names from a 40-name thematic screen (taxonomy v1.0). Long-duration growth multiples — expect drawdowns ~2x the value book's; sized as the SMALLEST sleeve by design and never blended into the Apex or Value NAVs.

macro_read: macro_regime.json reads NEUTRAL (score 0.55) with growth ACCELERATING but inflation ACCELERATING, rates neutral and credit stable — so against the +30-50%/12-mo goal I take a balanced-leaning-REACH stance: lean into the live earnings-momentum AI-capex chokepoints (TSM/NVDA at goal size, combined-capped) and the uranium/medtech/restaurant-OS re-rates that can deliver within a year, while accelerating inflation + a NEUTRAL tape cap how aggressive the reaches can be, so V and IDXX stay rate-duration/orthogonal ballast.
